article.title: Not join after accepting offer [FAAMG]
article.content: What happens if you accept an offer but decline just before the joining date because you got a better offer? Does the company blacklist you (officially or unofficially) or is there a cool down period? I'm talking about FAAMG companies like Amazon, Microsoft, Google? Is this even acceptable to do? Do recruiters refrain from hiring you? The better offer is a level up.

post.content: You tell them why you can\'t join. They will match/beat the competing offer if they want to. This happens quite often. You aren\'t blacklisted.

Take it from me, sometimes there is no choice. What if a better offer comes before joining? So it\u2019s the candidate\u2019s choice. They can\u2019t black list you. Give it a proper reason for not joining
I think you\'re rejecting MS for Google, but dont worry about getting blacklisted.
Nobody gives a damn.\nJust tell them that you have a better offer
